statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 15 of 27 countries showing critical levels in at least one fuel type, despite overall reserves above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
The EU's aggregate fuel reserves remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum across all fuel types, with petrol at 112.8 days, diesel at 101.5 days, and jet fuel at 95.1 days. However, this headline figure masks significant vulnerabilities at the national level, where more than half of member states are experiencing critical shortages in at least one fuel category.

The distribution of reserves reveals concerning patterns. Romania faces the most severe situation with critical levels across all three fuel types, including dangerously low diesel reserves of just 30.2 days. Poland, Luxembourg, and several other eastern European countries also show multiple critical indicators. Diesel shortages appear particularly widespread, affecting 15 of 27 countries at critical levels, while jet fuel reserves are critical in 13 countries.

Fuel prices reflect the ongoing supply pressures, with Brent crude oil holding steady at $109.26 per barrel—a 50.7% increase since late February's Middle East escalation. The price differential across member states is stark, with Netherlands petrol at €2.341 per liter compared to Malta's €1.34, indicating varying supply chain stresses and national tax policies. This data suggests the EU's fuel security system is functioning but under considerable strain from geopolitical supply disruptions.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 EU countries show critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with diesel most affected (15 countries critical)
Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types at critical levels, including diesel at just 30.2 days
Jet fuel reserves are particularly tight across the bloc, with 13 countries at critical levels and EU average at 95.1 days
Oil prices remain elevated at $109.26/barrel, up 50.7% since February's Middle East supply disruptions
Significant price variations exist across member states, from €1.34/L in Malta to €2.341/L in Netherlands for petrol
